questions.questionText: n moles of a non-volatile and non-electrolyte solute are dissolved in N moles of a solvent to form an ideal solution. If P is the vapour pressure of the solution and '$P^o$' is the vapour pressure of the pure solvent, then
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An ideal liquid mixture is prepared from two volatile liquids A ($p^\circ = 100$ torr) and B ($p^\circ = 200$ torr). The mixture has 1 mole of A and 4 mole of B. The total vapour pressure of the distillate will be :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A liquid is placed inside a closed container. A very light glass plate having a small hole is gently put over the liquid surface. The vapour pressure of the liquid inside the container will be:
options: Greater than the value obtained when the glass plate is not present
Equal to the value obtained when the glass plate is not present
Smaller than the value obtained when the glass plate is not present
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: The vapour pressure of water is determined by :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 50°C, pure benzene, C6H6, has a vapour pressure of 268 Torr. What number of moles of a non-volatile solute must be added per mole of benzene to obtain a benzene solution with vapour pressure 167 Torr at 50°C?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At places situated at high altitude, water starts boiling below $100^{\circ}$C due to
options: increase in the amount of heavy water
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At 350 K, $p_A^\circ = 300$ torr and $p_B^\circ = 800$ torr. The mole fraction of the mixture whose normal boiling point is 350 K is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: Let $P_0$ be the vapour pressure of the pure solvent and $P$ be the vapour pressure of its solution. If $N_1$ and $N_2$ denote the mole fractions of the solvent and the non-volatile solute respectively, the correct relation is :
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At the same temperature, the vapour pressure of a solution containing a non-volatile solute B in solvent A is 95% of the vapour pressure of pure solvent A. If the molar mass of the solvent is 0.3 times the molar mass of the solute, find the ratio of the mass of solvent to the mass of solute.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: An aqueous solution is made by dissolving 2 moles of [Cu(NH3)3Cl]Cl in 3 moles of H2O. Its relative lowering of vapour pressure is 0.50. On adding AgNO3, the solution will produce (assuming that the degree of ionisation of the substance does not change after adding AgNO3)
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: When 4.0 g of a non-volatile solute is dissolved in 54.0 g of water, the vapour pressure decreases by $1.0 \%$. Find the molar mass of the non-volatile solute.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: What mass of a non-volatile solute (molecular mass $=40$ ) must be dissolved in 114 g octane so that the vapour pressure becomes $80 \%$ of its original value?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: At temperature $T$, the vapour pressures of the pure liquids A and B, which give an ideal solution, are 300 torr and 800 torr respectively. A vapour mixture containing $A$ and $B$ has mole fraction of $A$ equal to 0.25 and is compressed slowly at the same temperature T. After the vapour condenses completely ($100 \%$ condensation), the vapour pressure of the liquid obtained is found to be ' $P$ '. Find the value of 684 - P.
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: How much mass of a solute (molecular mass 60) must be dissolved in 180 g of water so that the vapour pressure becomes $4 / 5^{\text {th }}$ of that of pure water?
questions.subject: Chemistry

questions.questionText: A solution made by adding 6 g of a non-volatile solute to 180 g of water has a vapour pressure of 20 torr. When 1 mole of water is added further to this solution, its vapour pressure rises by 0.02 torr. The molar mass (in $\mathrm{g} / \mathrm{mol}$ ) of the non-volatile solute is
questions.subject: Chemistry
